title: 9分达人听力6 Test 1 Listening
source: 《9分达人雅思听力真题还原及解析6》

groups.title: Community Centre Classes
groups.desc:
Questions 1-7
Complete the table below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.

questions.title: Saturday 【   】
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 Saturday；提示词 When、Saturday，此处应填时间类名词。
题目解析：表格题在定位和预判方面难度都不大。本题通过 Saturday 锁定答案句："Yes, it's on Saturday, and it will occupy the whole morning."注意抓取时间类名词即可。因此答案为 morning。

questions.title: 【   】 cooking class
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 cooking class；提示词 cooking class，此处应填形容词。
题目解析：本题不难。首先在咨询者的提问中听到 a class on cooking，随后工作人员回答："Yes, it's called French cooking class."其中 be called 意为"叫做"。因此答案为 French。

questions.title: the 【   】 Tuesday of every month
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 Anita Kitchen、Tuesday；提示词 When、the，此处应填序数词。
题目解析：听到 Anita Kitchen 出现后就需要注意与时间相关的描述了。本题存在干扰信息。定位词 Tuesday 第一次出现时可听到序数词 second，但注意录音中的 used to 表示过去，紧接着的转折信号词 but 告诉我们过去的情况有变、真正的时间即将出现："... the exact time will be changed to the first Tuesday."因此答案为 first。

questions.title: 【   】 to buy ingredients
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 apron、ingredients；提示词 What to bring、an apron，此处应填与 an apron 并列的名词。
题目解析：定位词 apron 先出现，接着通过并列信号词 What's more 预判答案即将出现，注意抓取附近的名词即可。答案句为："What's more, don't forget to bring some money with you ..."其中题干中的 bring 重现，另一个定位词 ingredients 在答案之后紧随出现。因此答案为 money。

questions.title: a 【   】 club
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 Beading class、club；提示词 Where、a、club，此处应填形容词或单数名词。
题目解析：听到 Beading class 出现后就需要注意与地点相关的描述了。本题存在干扰信息。定位词 club 第一次出现时可听到 tennis，但注意它对应的时间是 Last term，随后出现的 golf 对应的时间才是 this time。因此答案为 golf。

questions.title: 【   】 class
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 class；提示词 class，此处应填表示课程类型的名词。
题目解析：本题难度较低。首先根据咨询者的提问 "Um, do you have any other classes that I can join?"定位，接着工作人员回答："If you like, I think you can take our Painting class."因此答案为 Painting。

questions.title: own 【   】
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 What to bring；提示词 own，此处应填名词。
题目解析：本题通过咨询者的提问 "Do I have to bring the paints?"定位，随后工作人员回答："Well, paints will be provided by the tutor ..."故排除干扰信息 paints。之后听到："Um, the information says you'll just need some brushes."其中 need 替换了题干中的 bring，抓取其后的名词即可。因此答案为 brushes。

groups.title: Details of Beading Class
groups.desc:
Questions 8-10
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.
groups.collect:
The first class starts on: $8$
Cost of the class: $100
Room No.: $9$
Parking: next to a $10$

questions.title: The first class starts on: 【   】
questions.answer: May 15(th) / May 15th / May 15 / 15(th) May / 15th May / 15 May
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 first class；提示词 on，此处应填星期几或日期。
题目解析：定位词 first class 出现后，先听到了干扰信息 April 30th，提示本题考查的应该是日期，但需要注意的是这是报名的截止日期（the deadline for registration），之后出现的 May 15th 才是开课日期（start the class）。因此答案为 May 15(th) 或 15(th) May。

questions.title: Room No.: 【   】
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 Room；提示词 No.，此处应填数字或字母和数字的组合。
题目解析：本题较为简单。定位词 Room 原词重现，之后在工作人员的回答中直接抓取房间号即可，无干扰信息，但需注意区分 G 和 J 的发音。因此答案为 J52。

questions.title: Parking: next to a 【   】
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 Parking；提示词 next to、a，此处应填地点类单数名词。
题目解析：定位词 Parking 出现后，工作人员首先提到俱乐部的停车位只提供给高级会员，之后才由转折信号词 But 引出答案句："... a parking lot opposite the club, just beside the station."此处替换较简单，beside 替换了提示词 next to，其后名词即为答案。注意原句中的 opposite 与提示词 next to 不符，可排除干扰信息 club。因此答案为 station。

transcript.en: Assistant: Hello, this is Preston Community Centre.
transcript.cn: 你好，这里是普雷斯顿社区中心。

transcript.en: Man: Oh, hello.
transcript.cn: 哦，你好。

transcript.en: Man: My name's Andrew Shepherd.
transcript.cn: 我叫安德鲁·谢泼德。

transcript.en: Man: I'm ringing about the classes you offer.
transcript.cn: 我打电话是想问问你们开的课程。

transcript.en: Assistant: OK, Mr. Shepherd.
transcript.cn: 好的，谢泼德先生。

transcript.en: Assistant: Are there any classes in particular that you're interested in?
transcript.cn: 您有没有特别感兴趣的课程呢？

transcript.en: Man: Yes, you've got a class called Photography, I believe?
transcript.cn: 有的，我记得你们有一门叫摄影的课，对吧？

transcript.en: Assistant: That's right.
transcript.cn: 没错。

transcript.en: Assistant: It's the first time we offer this class because many people enquired about it last term.
transcript.cn: 这是我们第一次开这门课，因为上学期很多人来打听。

transcript.en: Assistant: It'll be held in a library not far from our community centre.
transcript.cn: 课会在离社区中心不远的一个图书馆里上。

transcript.en: Man: Right.
transcript.cn: 好的。

transcript.en: Man: I know it's on the weekend, right?
transcript.cn: 我知道是在周末上，对吧？

transcript.en: Assistant: Yes, it's on Saturday, and it will occupy the whole morning.
transcript.cn: 对，在周六，而且会占用一整个上午。

transcript.en: Man: OK.
transcript.cn: 好的。

transcript.en: Man: Um, what do I need to bring?
transcript.cn: 嗯，我需要带些什么？

transcript.en: Assistant: Obviously, you have to bring your own camera.
transcript.cn: 很显然，你得带上自己的相机。

transcript.en: Assistant: Some people bring a lot of accessories like an extra lens, but there's really no need for this class.
transcript.cn: 有些人会带很多配件，比如额外的镜头，但这门课其实真的用不着。

transcript.en: Assistant: It's mainly focusing on composition really, and getting the most out of the basic camera.
transcript.cn: 它主要讲的是构图，以及怎么把一台基础相机用到极致。

transcript.en: Man: That's exactly what I want.
transcript.cn: 这正是我想要的。

transcript.en: Man: OK, then I've heard there's a class on cooking, right?
transcript.cn: 好的，那我听说还有一门烹饪课，对吧？

transcript.en: Assistant: Yes, it's called French cooking class.
transcript.cn: 对，叫法式烹饪课。

transcript.en: Assistant: As the name suggests, it will teach you cooking skills in French cuisine.
transcript.cn: 顾名思义，它会教你法国菜的烹饪技巧。

transcript.en: Man: Oh, great.
transcript.cn: 哦，太好了。

transcript.en: Man: Where will that be held?
transcript.cn: 那门课在哪儿上呢？

transcript.en: Assistant: This class is special, so we arrange it in a kitchen where we can get the necessary cooking equipment.
transcript.cn: 这门课比较特殊，所以我们安排在一个厨房里，那里能有必要的烹饪设备。

transcript.en: Assistant: Let me see, yes, the Anita Kitchen.
transcript.cn: 我看看，对，是安妮塔厨房。

transcript.en: Assistant: As for the time of the course, it used to be on the second Tuesday of every month, but there's a small adjustment this time.
transcript.cn: 至于上课时间，以前是每个月第二个周二，不过这次有个小调整。

transcript.en: Assistant: Although it's still held on a monthly basis, the exact time will be changed to the first Tuesday.
transcript.cn: 虽然还是每月上一次，但具体时间会改到第一个周二。

transcript.en: Man: Well, actually it's my wife who's really interested, and I think it's a good fit for her because our daughter has to take a guitar lesson every Tuesday, so my wife is available.
transcript.cn: 其实真正感兴趣的是我太太，我觉得挺适合她的，因为我们女儿每周二都要上吉他课，所以我太太正好有空。

transcript.en: Assistant: That's good.
transcript.cn: 那挺好的。

transcript.en: Assistant: But I have to remind you that aprons are not provided in this class, so when you do come, please bring one with you in case you get dirty when cooking.
transcript.cn: 不过我得提醒您一下，这门课不提供围裙，所以来的时候请自己带一条，免得做饭时弄脏衣服。

transcript.en: Assistant: What's more, don't forget to bring some money with you, as the ingredients for cooking, like vegetables or meat, have to be paid by yourself.
transcript.cn: 另外，别忘了带点钱，因为做菜用的食材，比如蔬菜或肉，都得您自己出钱买。

transcript.en: Man: OK, useful information to know.
transcript.cn: 好的，这些信息很有用。

transcript.en: Man: Then there's another class that my wife would like to join – that's the Beading class.
transcript.cn: 然后还有一门课我太太想报——就是串珠课。

transcript.en: Assistant: Oh, I've taken the class myself.
transcript.cn: 哦，我自己上过这门课。

transcript.en: Assistant: The tutor is excellent.
transcript.cn: 那位老师特别棒。

transcript.en: Assistant: Last term it was held in a tennis club, which is going to be redecorated soon, so we plan to change this class to a golf club this time.
transcript.cn: 上学期是在一个网球俱乐部上的，那儿马上要重新装修了，所以这次我们打算把课改到一个高尔夫俱乐部。

transcript.en: Assistant: And it'll be held on the evening of every Wednesday.
transcript.cn: 课会在每周三晚上上。

transcript.en: Man: Do I have to bring the beads with me?
transcript.cn: 我需要自己带珠子吗？

transcript.en: Assistant: Yes, that's the only material needed for this class.
transcript.cn: 是的，那是这门课唯一要用到的材料。

transcript.en: Man: OK, I think this class is also a good choice for her.
transcript.cn: 好的，我觉得这门课对她来说也是个不错的选择。

transcript.en: Man: Um, do you have any other classes that I can join?
transcript.cn: 嗯，你们还有其他我能报的课吗？

transcript.en: Assistant: If you like, I think you can take our Painting class.
transcript.cn: 如果您愿意的话，我觉得您可以上我们的绘画课。

transcript.en: Assistant: It's a popular class, so this term we're going to move it to a high school so that we get more room.
transcript.cn: 这门课很受欢迎，所以这学期我们打算把它挪到一所高中去上，这样场地能大一些。

transcript.en: Man: What time will it be?
transcript.cn: 上课时间是什么时候？

transcript.en: Assistant: It'll be on every Monday evening.
transcript.cn: 在每周一晚上。

transcript.en: Man: The time is good for me.
transcript.cn: 这个时间对我挺合适。

transcript.en: Man: Do I have to bring the paints?
transcript.cn: 我需要自己带颜料吗？

transcript.en: Assistant: Well, paints will be provided by the tutor, I know that.
transcript.cn: 嗯，颜料会由老师提供，这个我知道。

transcript.en: Assistant: Um, the information says you'll just need some brushes.
transcript.cn: 嗯，资料上说你只需要带几支画笔就行。

transcript.en: Man: I see.
transcript.cn: 我明白了。

transcript.en: Man: OK, just another question about the Beading class.
transcript.cn: 好的，关于串珠课我还有个问题。

transcript.en: Man: I've just remembered my wife asked me to find out the exact time – when will the first class begin?
transcript.cn: 我刚想起来我太太让我问清楚具体时间——第一节课什么时候开始？

transcript.en: Assistant: Well, the deadline for registration is April 30th, and we are not going to start the class until May 15th.
transcript.cn: 嗯，报名截止日期是4月30日，我们要到5月15日才开课。

transcript.en: Man: OK.
transcript.cn: 好的。

transcript.en: Man: What's the fee for this class?
transcript.cn: 这门课的费用是多少？

transcript.en: Assistant: It was 95 dollars last term, but the cost will be a little more this term.
transcript.cn: 上学期是95美元，不过这学期会稍微贵一点。

transcript.en: Assistant: Well, let me check the details.
transcript.cn: 嗯，我查一下具体情况。

transcript.en: Assistant: OK, it'll be 100 dollars this term, just 5 dollars more.
transcript.cn: 好的，这学期是100美元，就多了5美元。

transcript.en: Man: Sounds reasonable.
transcript.cn: 听起来挺合理的。

transcript.en: Man: Then about the location.
transcript.cn: 那再说说地点吧。

transcript.en: Man: It's in the club, which room exactly?
transcript.cn: 是在那个俱乐部里，具体是哪个房间？

transcript.en: Assistant: Room J52.
transcript.cn: J52房间。

transcript.en: Man: OK, just one more question.
transcript.cn: 好的，还有一个问题。

transcript.en: Man: Is parking convenient?
transcript.cn: 停车方便吗？

transcript.en: Assistant: I'm sorry to tell you that parking space is only available for the club's premier members.
transcript.cn: 很抱歉地告诉您，停车位只对俱乐部的高级会员开放。

transcript.en: Assistant: But if you drive, there's a parking lot opposite the club, just beside the station.
transcript.cn: 不过如果您开车来，俱乐部对面有个停车场，就在车站旁边。

transcript.en: Assistant: You need to pay only a small parking fee.
transcript.cn: 您只需付一点点停车费就行。

transcript.en: Man: OK.
transcript.cn: 好的。

transcript.en: Man: That's all I want to know.
transcript.cn: 我想了解的就这些。

transcript.en: Man: I'll call you later after I discuss it with my wife.
transcript.cn: 我跟太太商量一下再给你们回电话。

transcript.en: Man: Thank you very much.
transcript.cn: 非常感谢。

transcript.en: Assistant: You're welcome.
transcript.cn: 不客气。

transcript.en: Assistant: Bye!
transcript.cn: 再见！

transcript.en: Man: Bye!
transcript.cn: 再见！

groups.desc:
Questions 11-15
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.title: What does Darren now think about the Latin name of the red squirrel?

options.title: A
options.content: It is suitable for this species.

questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 Latin name；提示词 now。
题目解析：提示词 now 为条件限定常见考点，听时需关注时间。选项 A 和 C 均表示积极态度，选项 B 表示消极态度，本题需结合态度听。在听到定位词 Latin name 时就要集中注意力听后面的句子，首先听到："At first, I thought this description was funny and was a kind of exaggeration." 其中 funny 替换了选项 C 中的 amusing，但本句的时间限定 At first 与题干 now 不符，故排除选项 C。接着听到："But now I realize that this name suits them very well." 其中 suits them very well 替换了选项 A 中的 is suitable for this species，同时时间限定 now 符合题干要求。选项 B 在录音中未提及。因此答案为 A。

questions.title: The number of red squirrels is reduced due to

options.title: A
options.content: the invasion of an exotic species.

options.title: B
options.content: a disease brought by the exotic species.

questions.analysis:
听前预测：提示词 number、reduced。
题目解析：本题没有很好的定位词，审题时可在题干处标画向下箭头"↓"表示"数量下降"，听时注意抓取信息方向。选项 A 和 B 都有 exotic species 这一表述，二者区别在于 invasion 和 disease 这两个信息，可圈画这两个词进行选项区分。录音中先是出现了 decreasing number in the wild，暗示跟"数量下降"相关的信息即将出现。紧接着听到 exotic species 出现，不过只是在陈述一个事实，即在外来物种被引进时，红松鼠当时仅存 2 万只，并不能因此推断出红松鼠的数量下降与外来物种的引进有关，故排除选项 A。随后听到 disease 出现，提到这种病毒可以导致红松鼠的死亡，但紧接着出现了转折信号词 But，解释红松鼠的数量在疾病传播前就下降了（had already declined before ...），故排除选项 B。与选项 C 中 food 相关的描述随后出现，在录音中被具体替换为 nuts，且录音中的 shrinking population 表述符合题干"数量下降"这一信息方向，primary food source 对应选项 C 中的 supply。因此答案为 C。

questions.title: Darren differentiates red squirrels from rats by
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 rats；提示词 differentiates。
题目解析：三个选项中都有 food 一词，注意区分选项信息：A 食物类别，B 吃的方式，C 储存方式。定位词 rats 出现后听到："... as they have a very similar food category to red squirrels." 其中 similar 与题干 differentiates 矛盾，故排除选项 A。随后听到："But I knew it was red squirrels because of their different feeding habits." 其中 different feeding habits 为选项 B 的同义替换，后一句则为 different feeding habits 的具体描述。选项 C 在录音中未提及。因此答案为 B。

questions.title: What was the restriction for Darren's photography?

options.title: A
options.content: He could only take photos of one species at a time.

options.title: B
options.content: He could only use one camera.

options.title: C
options.content: He could only take photographs in a certain location.

questions.analysis:
听前预测：提示词 restriction。
题目解析：题干的条件限定为 restriction，比较容易发生替换。审题时可标画"↓"箭头，重点听负向态度信息。三个选项的区别在于：A 一次一种动物，B 一台相机，C 一个特定地点。录音中出现了负向态度信息 Unfortunately，符合题干目标信息方向，注意听随后的内容："... I had to shoot only in my garden, ..." 其中 shoot only 替换了选项 C 中的 only take photographs，my garden 为选项 C 中的 a certain location 的具体展开替换。之后提到其他动物有时也会入镜，可排除选项 A。录音中未提及对相机数量的限制，排除选项 B。因此答案为 C。

questions.title: Why is Darren confident about his photographs?
questions.analysis:
听前预测：提示词 confident。
题目解析：题干的条件限定为 confident，可标画"↑"箭头，重点听积极态度信息。录音中出现了积极态度信息 more than satisfied，符合题干目标信息方向，随后出现了一个由转折信号词 Even though 引导的让步状语从句，注意该词出现时，从句为否定的信息，主句才是说话人的重点。从句中提到练习得很少，且只有一台来自朋友的二手相机，显然与选项 A 和 C 表达的态度相反，可排除。主句中提到导师在摄影上帮了很大的忙，且随后再次强调自己从中获得了指导，能够更好地摄影，其中 mentor（导师）和 tutorials（辅导）为选项 B 中 teacher 的具体展开替换。因此答案为 B。

groups.desc:
Questions 16-20
Complete the sentences below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.
groups.collect: The photos will be exhibited in a $16$. Darren's photos once won a competition held by a $17$. Ways to improve photography skills: Some pictures need a so-called '$18$' in the scene. Darren thinks the lighting is best late in the $19$. To take better photos, photographers should make $20$.

questions.title: The photos will be exhibited in a 【   】.
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 exhibited；提示词 a，此处应填（单数）名词或名词短语。
题目解析：注意 photos 是全篇的主题词，无法起到很好的定位作用。题干限定最多两个单词，意味着有可能是一个名词，也可能是一个名词短语。定位词 exhibited 在录音中被替换为 on display。答案出现得较快，需要我们注意力高度集中。另外，在填写答案时注意在符合题干字数要求的情况下保证信息的完整性。因此答案为 local museum。

questions.title: Darren's photos once won a competition held by a 【   】.
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 competition；提示词 a，此处应填（单数）名词或名词短语。
题目解析：本题难度不高，通过定位词 competition 比较容易识别答案位置："I sent my portfolio for the competition hosted by a national newspaper." 其中 hosted 替换了题干中的 held。因此答案为 national newspaper。注意填写完整的答案信息。

questions.title: Some pictures need a so-called '【   】' in the scene.
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 scene；提示词 a、so-called，此处应填（单数）名词或名词短语，可能为摄影类术语或专有名称。
题目解析：在听到小标题中的 photography skills 时，注意与题干相关的内容即将出现，两三句无效信息之后出现了含定位词 scene 的答案句："In order to achieve balance, a 'Secondary Object', so to speak, can be added to the scene at the opposing intersection." 其中 so to speak 替换了题干中的 so-called，符合答案预判且代入题干之后合理的词汇只有 Secondary Object。因此答案为 Secondary Object。注意单词拼写，secondary 意为"第二的"，在教育相关场景中常出现 secondary school，意为"高中"。

questions.title: Darren thinks the lighting is best late in the 【   】.
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 lighting、late；提示词 the，此处应填名词或名词短语。
题目解析：注意 best 为条件限定。首先听到："Lighting is another essential element ..." 此时可知后面要讲 lighting 的相关信息，应集中注意力。之后听到："... lighting is most ideal in the late afternoon." 其中 most ideal 替换了题干中的 best，late 原词重现，抓取其后的名词即可。因此答案为 afternoon。

questions.title: To take better photos, photographers should make 【   】.
questions.analysis:
听前预测：提示词 make，此处应填名词或名词短语。
题目解析：本题没有很好的定位词，注意 better 为条件限定，可标画"↑"箭头，关注积极态度信息。录音中出现的 further improve on 符合积极态度信息，注意听随后的内容："... it is advised to take detailed notes of timing ..." 其中 take 替换了题干中的 make，其后的名词短语即为答案。因此答案为 detailed notes。注意 detailed 中 d 的发音会存在失去爆破的情况，d 弱读，需要结合语法知识判断此处为形容词 -ed 形式。

transcript.en: Woman: Good afternoon, our beloved listeners.
transcript.cn: 各位亲爱的听众，下午好。

transcript.en: Woman: Welcome to today's Capturing the Moment programme.
transcript.cn: 欢迎收听今天的《捕捉瞬间》节目。

transcript.en: Woman: Today, we have Darren Almond here with us.
transcript.cn: 今天，达伦·阿尔蒙德来到了我们的节目现场。

transcript.en: Woman: He's a college student who has just won an award for his set of shots of red squirrels.
transcript.cn: 他是一名大学生，刚刚凭借一组红松鼠的照片获了奖。

transcript.en: Woman: Welcome, Darren.
transcript.cn: 欢迎你，达伦。

transcript.en: Woman: Now, can you tell us a bit about this lovely creature and how you managed to take such wonderful photos?
transcript.cn: 那么，你能跟我们讲讲这种可爱的小动物，以及你是怎么拍出这么棒的照片的吗？

transcript.en: Darren: Well, the red squirrel is Britain's only native squirrel.
transcript.cn: 嗯，红松鼠是英国唯一的本土松鼠。

transcript.en: Darren: It's graceful and athletic.
transcript.cn: 它优雅又灵活。

transcript.en: Darren: Its Latin name 'Sciurus Vulgaris' comes from the Greek words, describing a common type of animal which sits in the shadow of its own tail.
transcript.cn: 它的拉丁名'Sciurus Vulgaris'来自希腊语，形容一种常见的、坐在自己尾巴阴影下的动物。

transcript.en: Darren: At first, I thought this description was funny and was a kind of exaggeration.
transcript.cn: 一开始，我觉得这个描述挺好笑的，像是一种夸张。

transcript.en: Darren: But now I realize that this name suits them very well.
transcript.cn: 但现在我意识到，这个名字非常贴切。

transcript.en: Darren: Their fluffy tails under the sun normally cast a big shadow over themselves.
transcript.cn: 它们蓬松的尾巴在阳光下往往会在自己身上投下一大片阴影。

transcript.en: Darren: You will be lucky to see a red squirrel in the areas where they still survive, due to its timid nature and decreasing number in the wild.
transcript.cn: 由于红松鼠生性胆小、野外数量又在减少，你能在它们还幸存的地区看到一只，那可算是运气不错。

transcript.en: Darren: In the late 1800s when grey squirrels, an exotic species, were first introduced into Britain, there were only about 20,000 red squirrels left, the majority of which lived in the north of England.
transcript.cn: 在19世纪末，当灰松鼠这种外来物种首次被引入英国时，红松鼠只剩下大约两万只，其中大多数生活在英格兰北部。

transcript.en: Darren: The greys carry a disease called the squirrelpox virus which could lead to the death of red squirrels.
transcript.cn: 灰松鼠携带一种叫松鼠痘病毒的疾病，可能导致红松鼠死亡。

transcript.en: Darren: But their number had already declined before the disease spread there.
transcript.cn: 但在这种病传播过去之前，它们的数量其实就已经在下降了。

transcript.en: Darren: The shrinking population actually corresponds to decreases in the number of nuts available, the red squirrels' primary food source.
transcript.cn: 数量的减少其实和坚果供应量的下降相吻合，而坚果正是红松鼠的主要食物来源。

transcript.en: Darren: I accidentally left some nuts in my garden one day.
transcript.cn: 有一天我不小心把一些坚果落在了自家花园里。

transcript.en: Darren: And I found they had been eaten the next morning.
transcript.cn: 结果第二天早上我发现它们被吃掉了。

transcript.en: Darren: It could have been rats as they have a very similar food category to red squirrels.
transcript.cn: 本来可能是老鼠吃的，因为它们和红松鼠的食物种类很相似。

transcript.en: Darren: But I knew it was red squirrels because of their different feeding habits.
transcript.cn: 但我知道那是红松鼠，因为它们的进食习惯不一样。

transcript.en: Darren: Rats bite a hole in the nuts, whereas red squirrels crack the nuts in half and eat the kernels.
transcript.cn: 老鼠会在坚果上咬个洞，而红松鼠会把坚果掰成两半，吃里面的果仁。

transcript.en: Darren: After a week or so, the red squirrels became regular visitors to my garden, making them ideal subjects for my photography project.
transcript.cn: 大概过了一个星期，红松鼠就成了我花园的常客，成了我摄影项目的理想拍摄对象。

transcript.en: Darren: Unfortunately, I had to shoot only in my garden, partly because I couldn't find another spot to sight red squirrels on a regular basis.
transcript.cn: 可惜，我只能在自家花园里拍，一方面是因为我找不到别的能稳定看到红松鼠的地方。

transcript.en: Darren: Also, this could block out any outside distractions, and I could focus solely on my subject.
transcript.cn: 另一方面，这样也能屏蔽掉外界的干扰，让我可以专注于拍摄对象。

transcript.en: Darren: During the shooting, other animals like birds got caught in the frame together sometimes.
transcript.cn: 拍摄过程中，有时鸟之类的其他动物也会一起入镜。

transcript.en: Darren: I'm more than satisfied with my photos.
transcript.cn: 我对自己的照片非常满意。

transcript.en: Darren: Even though I only practiced a few times and used just a second-hand camera from my friend, my great mentor helped me with the shooting.
transcript.cn: 尽管我只练习了几次，用的还只是朋友的一台二手相机，但我那位了不起的导师在拍摄上帮了我很多。

transcript.en: Darren: I got great tutorials from him and was able to shoot better photos.
transcript.cn: 我从他那儿得到了很好的指导，才能拍出更好的照片。

transcript.en: Woman: Thank you, Darren.
transcript.cn: 谢谢你，达伦。

transcript.en: Woman: The award-winning photos will be on display in a local museum for the coming month.
transcript.cn: 这些获奖照片将在接下来的一个月在当地一家博物馆展出。

transcript.en: Woman: We are looking forward to the exhibition.
transcript.cn: 我们很期待这次展览。

transcript.en: Woman: So, tell us about the award.
transcript.cn: 那，跟我们说说这个奖吧。

transcript.en: Darren: OK.
transcript.cn: 好的。

transcript.en: Darren: I sent my portfolio for the competition hosted by a national newspaper.
transcript.cn: 我把我的作品集投给了一家全国性报纸举办的比赛。

transcript.en: Darren: I wasn't expecting the third prize.
transcript.cn: 我本来没指望能拿三等奖。

transcript.en: Darren: So, when they notified me of the result on the phone, I was really excited.
transcript.cn: 所以当他们打电话通知我结果时，我真的特别激动。

transcript.en: Woman: Do you have some tips for our audience to help improve their photography skills?
transcript.cn: 你有什么技巧能分享给我们的听众，帮他们提升摄影水平吗？

transcript.en: Darren: Of course.
transcript.cn: 当然有。

transcript.en: Darren: Here are three things I think photographers can work on to get a better shot.
transcript.cn: 这里有三点我觉得摄影师可以下功夫、从而拍出更好照片的地方。

transcript.en: Darren: First of all, good composition is key to a lovely shot.
transcript.cn: 首先，好的构图是拍出一张好照片的关键。

transcript.en: Darren: Normally, an image's centre of interest is placed at one of the intersections that trisect the whole picture.
transcript.cn: 通常，一张图片的兴趣中心会放在把整幅画面三等分的交叉点之一上。

transcript.en: Darren: In order to achieve balance, a 'Secondary Object', so to speak, can be added to the scene at the opposing intersection.
transcript.cn: 为了达到平衡，可以说，可以在画面另一侧的交叉点上加一个'次要物体'。

transcript.en: Darren: So, for instance, in one of my shots, a red squirrel at the top left is staring at a nut at the lower right corner.
transcript.cn: 比如说，在我的一张照片里，左上角有一只红松鼠，正盯着右下角的一颗坚果。

transcript.en: Darren: This is called 'the Rule of Thirds'.
transcript.cn: 这就叫'三分法则'。

transcript.en: Darren: Lighting is another essential element if you want to be rewarded with a superb photo.
transcript.cn: 如果你想拍出一张出色的照片，光线是另一个关键要素。

transcript.en: Darren: The direction of the light falling on your subject is most important of all.
transcript.cn: 光线照在拍摄对象上的方向是最重要的。

transcript.en: Darren: You need to look at your subject carefully and watch how the shadows fall.
transcript.cn: 你得仔细观察你的拍摄对象，留意阴影是怎么落下来的。

transcript.en: Darren: If you're able to choose the time of day to shoot your pictures, lighting is most ideal in the late afternoon.
transcript.cn: 如果你能选择拍摄的时间段，那么傍晚时分的光线最理想。

transcript.en: Darren: Try to position yourself so that the sun hits your subject from the side.
transcript.cn: 尽量调整自己的位置，让阳光从侧面照到拍摄对象上。

transcript.en: Darren: This helps create a 3D effect in the picture.
transcript.cn: 这有助于在画面里营造出立体感。

transcript.en: Darren: To further improve on your photography skills, it is advised to take detailed notes of timing, position, weather, etc.
transcript.cn: 要进一步提升摄影水平，建议你把时间、位置、天气等细节都详细记下来。

transcript.en: Darren: By doing so, it is easier to review and adjust accordingly, and there is always room for improvement.
transcript.cn: 这样做，回顾和相应调整都会更容易，而且总是有改进的空间。

groups.question_title: Which TWO problems are caused by water hyacinth?
groups.desc:
Questions 21 and 22
Choose TWO letters, A-E.

questions.analysis:
听前预测：提示词 problems。
题目解析：注意 water hyacinth 是全篇的主题词，难以用于定位。题干的条件限定为 problems，审题时可标画“↓”箭头，重点听负向态度信息。录音中从 worst 开始出现负向态度，随后听到了 problems to the local people，但没提及中毒，排除选项 D。选项 B 中的 Farmers 紧接着出现，提到了他们不能划船，因此无法捕鱼，故选项 B 正确。选项 C 中的 dam 出现，但未提及它的 structure 如何，而是说它常因为发电机涡轮叶片被水葫芦的叶子缠住而停止运转，从而严重限制了电能供给，选项 E 信息较为完整，更符合录音中的描述，优先选择 E；录音中的 electricity generation 对应选项 E 中的 Electricity production，severely restricting 则对应 is affected。选项 A 中的 Plants 在录音中被替换为 algae，fish 原词出现，不过同样未提及中毒。因此答案为 B & E。

听前预测：提示词 problems。
题目解析：注意 water hyacinth 是全篇的主题词，难以用于定位。题干的条件限定为 problems，审题时可标画“↓”箭头，重点听负向态度信息。录音中从 worst 开始出现负向态度，随后听到了 problems to the local people，但没提及中毒，排除选项 D。选项 B 中的 Farmers 紧接着出现，提到了他们不能划船，因此无法捕鱼，故选项 B 正确。选项 C 中的 dam 出现，但未提及它的 structure 如何，而是说它常因为发电机涡轮叶片被水葫芦的叶子缠住而停止运转，从而严重限制了电能供给，选项 E 信息较为完整，更符合录音中的描述，优先选择 E；录音中的 electricity generation 对应选项 E 中的 Electricity production，severely restricting 则对应 is affected。选项 A 中的 Plants 在录音中被替换为 algae，fish 原词出现，不过同样未提及中毒。因此答案为 B & E。

groups.desc:
Questions 23-26
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.title: Where was water hyacinth originally from?
questions.analysis:
听前预测：提示词 Where、originally from。
题目解析：本题题干句式与录音原文基本一致，比较容易定位，答案出现得较快。be originally from 在录音中被替换为 be native to，其后的 Latin America 即为最初来源地。因此答案为 A。

questions.title: What is the primary cause of the decrease in nutrients from the soil?
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 nutrients、soil；提示词 primary cause、decrease。
题目解析：本题极具迷惑性。在 nutrients 出现后听到了“Why is that?”，提示接下来的是原因，需要集中注意力听。先是听到：“The main reason for soil nutrient loss is that local residents cut down trees.”其中 main reason 替换了题干中的 primary cause，soil nutrient loss 替换了 decrease in nutrients from the soil，故选项 C 正确。之后出现了干扰信息 soil erosion 和 frequent rain，分别对应选项 A 和 B，但 indirectly 和 partly 说明它们不符合题干限定 primary cause。因此答案为 C。

questions.title: When will the biological solution bring risks to the environment?
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 biological solution、environment；提示词 risks。
题目解析：题干的条件限定为 risks，审题时可标画“↓”箭头，重点听负向态度信息。选项 B 中的 6 months 和选项 A 中的 immediately 先后出现，不过都与 risks 无关。接着录音中出现了负向态度信息 side effects，符合题干目标信息方向，注意听随后的内容：“... of this method on local ecology will remain unforeseen for years ...”其中 this method 指的就是题干中的 biological solution，local ecology 对应题干中的 environment，remain ... for years 替换了选项 C 的 many years later。因此答案为 C。

questions.title: What does John say about the mechanical solution?
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 mechanical solution；提示词 John。
题目解析：本题的三个选项均为负向信息，在听到定位词 mechanical solution 后，注意关注男生所表达的负向态度。录音中从 however 开始出现负向信息：“It is, however, inefficient for workers to process extensive areas, and costly to use large cutting and dredging equipment. Ironically, it costs even more to dispose of this plant than to remove it.”其中 inefficient 意为“低效的”，注意与选项 A 的 ineffective（无效的）进行区分。costly、costs even more 都表达了选项 C 的 expensive 含义。因此答案为 C。

groups.question_title: What is the benefit of the cut-down water hyacinth to each of the following aspects?
groups.desc:
Questions 27-30
What is the benefit of the cut-down water hyacinth to each of the following aspects?
Choose FOUR answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-F, next to Questions 27-30.

collect_option.title: Benefits of the cut-down water hyacinth

list.title: A
list.content: can reduce the effect of global warming

list.title: B
list.content: can be used as an alternative energy resource

list.title: D
list.content: can produce materials for plants to grow in

list.title: E
list.content: can be good for human health

questions.title: dried water hyacinth
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 dried water hyacinth。
题目解析：定位词 dried water hyacinth 在录音中被替换为 water hyacinth is left to dry，但比较容易识别。之后听到：“The mixture can be used as alternative soil rather than fertiliser. It can grow crops with increased yield.”其中 alternative soil 为选项 D 中 materials 的具体展开替换，crops 对应 plants。虽然选项 F 中的 fertiliser 有提及，但通过否定信号词 rather than 可排除。因此答案为 D。

questions.title: mushroom farmers
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 mushroom farmers。
题目解析：定位词 mushroom farmers 在录音中原词重现，随后出现答案相关信息：“Using the mixture mentioned earlier, mushrooms generally grow much faster so that farmers can harvest mushrooms sooner, which enables them to make money faster.”其中 make money faster 替换了选项 C 中的 make quick profits。因此答案为 C。

questions.title: oyster and straw mushrooms
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 oyster and straw mushrooms。
题目解析：定位词 oyster and straw mushrooms 原词重现于女生的提问中，随后男生进行了详细阐述。接着听到：“These mushrooms provide sufficient nutritional values including minerals and high-quality protein which are essential to people's level of fitness.”其中 nutritional values、minerals and high-quality protein 和 essential to people's level of fitness 与选项 E 中的 good for human health 对应。因此答案为 E。

questions.title: cows
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 cows。
题目解析：定位词 cows 同样原词重现，答案句随即出现：“Then the waste from cows is treated to produce methane gas, which can be used as fuel for cooking, lighting or powering an engine.”其中 methane gas 意为“沼气”，并提及这是一种燃料，它是选项 B 中的 alternative energy 的具体展开替换。虽然干扰选项 A 中的 global warming 在后一句中被提及，但录音中并未明确表示水葫芦可用于减少全球变暖，所以可排除。因此答案为 B。

transcript.en: Arya: John, how is it going with your research on water hyacinth?
transcript.cn: 约翰，你那个关于水葫芦的研究进展怎么样了？

transcript.en: John: Well, I went on a field trip on my own and collected the primary data.
transcript.cn: 嗯，我自己去做了一次实地考察，收集了一手数据。

transcript.en: John: After I came back, I also referred to the published data.
transcript.cn: 回来之后，我还查阅了一些已发表的数据。

transcript.en: Arya: So what do you know about this plant?
transcript.cn: 那你对这种植物都了解到了些什么？

transcript.en: John: Water hyacinth grows in tropical countries.
transcript.cn: 水葫芦生长在热带国家。

transcript.en: John: It has beautiful purple-blue flowers, but everybody hates it.
transcript.cn: 它开着漂亮的紫蓝色花，但大家都讨厌它。

transcript.en: Arya: Why is that?
transcript.cn: 为什么呢？

transcript.en: John: Water hyacinth, as the world's worst invasive aquatic plant, has attracted significant attention.
transcript.cn: 水葫芦作为世界上最恶劣的入侵性水生植物，引起了广泛关注。

transcript.en: John: Millions of these plants grow in rivers and lakes and have brought huge environmental and cultural problems to the local people.
transcript.cn: 数以百万计的这种植物长在河流和湖泊里，给当地人带来了巨大的环境和文化问题。

transcript.en: John: Farmers cannot travel in boats on the water, and thus cannot fish in it.
transcript.cn: 农民没法划船在水上通行，因此也没法在水里捕鱼。

transcript.en: John: In addition, heavy free-floating mats of water hyacinth are clogging the dam at the power station in West Africa.
transcript.cn: 此外，成片自由漂浮的水葫芦正在堵塞西非发电站的水坝。

transcript.en: John: Turbines of the power station are often shut down because of the intertwined leaves.
transcript.cn: 发电站的涡轮机常常因为缠绕在一起的叶子而被迫停机。

transcript.en: John: Dams often cease to function as a result, severely restricting the electricity generation.
transcript.cn: 结果水坝常常无法运转，严重限制了发电。

transcript.en: John: Dense mats of water hyacinth also shade the surface and prevent algae growth, thus limiting the production of oxygen and threatening the survival of fish.
transcript.cn: 密密麻麻的水葫芦还会遮住水面，阻碍藻类生长，从而限制氧气的产生，威胁到鱼类的生存。

transcript.en: Arya: So where did water hyacinth originate from?
transcript.cn: 那水葫芦最初是从哪里来的呢？

transcript.en: John: Water hyacinth is native to Amazon Basin in Latin America.
transcript.cn: 水葫芦原产于拉丁美洲的亚马逊河流域。

transcript.en: John: It has spread mainly to the tropics and subtropics throughout much of the world since the 1800s.
transcript.cn: 自19世纪以来，它主要扩散到了世界上大部分的热带和亚热带地区。

transcript.en: John: Later, it was introduced into Europe and Africa as an ornamental garden pond plant due to its beauty.
transcript.cn: 后来，因为它长得漂亮，被作为观赏性的花园池塘植物引入了欧洲和非洲。

transcript.en: John: Flowers of this plant were being sold in local markets in African countries as a source of income for women.
transcript.cn: 在一些非洲国家，这种植物的花会在当地市场上出售，成为妇女的一种收入来源。

transcript.en: John: Since the 1900s, this weed has infested lakes, rivers and creeks of the delta areas in West Africa.
transcript.cn: 自20世纪以来，这种杂草侵占了西非三角洲地区的湖泊、河流和小溪。

transcript.en: Arya: I guess whoever introduced this plant had never expected the disastrous impact it would have on the local ecological environment.
transcript.cn: 我猜当初引进这种植物的人，绝对没料到它会给当地生态环境带来这么灾难性的影响。

transcript.en: Arya: What kind of habitat does water hyacinth thrive most in?
transcript.cn: 水葫芦在什么样的环境里长得最旺盛？

transcript.en: John: Well, it has been discovered that the nutrient-rich environment contributes significantly to the rapid proliferation of this weed, which obtains its nutrients directly from the water.
transcript.cn: 嗯，研究发现，富含养分的环境对这种杂草的快速繁殖起了很大作用，它直接从水中获取养分。

transcript.en: John: This is normally compounded with a decrease of nutrients along the river banks.
transcript.cn: 而这通常还伴随着河岸沿线养分的减少。

transcript.en: Arya: Why is that?
transcript.cn: 这是为什么呢？

transcript.en: John: The main reason for soil nutrient loss is that local residents cut down trees.
transcript.cn: 土壤养分流失的主要原因是当地居民砍伐树木。

transcript.en: John: Excessive deforestation accelerates soil erosion, which indirectly compounds this issue.
transcript.cn: 过度砍伐加速了水土流失，间接地加剧了这个问题。

transcript.en: John: Without enough trees to retain nutrients in the soil, frequent rain might partly aggravate this by carrying some of the nutrients into the waterways.
transcript.cn: 没有足够的树木来保持土壤中的养分，频繁的降雨可能会把一部分养分冲进水道，从而在一定程度上加重这一状况。

transcript.en: Arya: Are there any effective measures to control or even eradicate this weed?
transcript.cn: 有没有什么有效的办法来控制、甚至根除这种杂草？

transcript.en: John: Yes, a number of approaches have been widely applied throughout the world.
transcript.cn: 有，世界各地已经广泛采用了好几种方法。

transcript.en: John: Among them, biological control is the most widely favoured long-term control method by introducing a type of insect that feeds on water hyacinth.
transcript.cn: 其中，生物防治是最受青睐的长期防治方法，做法是引入一种以水葫芦为食的昆虫。

transcript.en: John: So far, Nigeria has used this method for 6 months.
transcript.cn: 到目前为止，尼日利亚用这种方法已经六个月了。

transcript.en: John: However, water hyacinth won't be removed immediately.
transcript.cn: 不过，水葫芦并不会马上被清除掉。

transcript.en: John: The side effects of this method on local ecology will remain unforeseen for years because it takes time for this external insect species to reach a density sufficient enough to affect ecological development.
transcript.cn: 这种方法对当地生态的副作用要好多年后才会显现出来，因为这种外来昆虫需要时间才能达到足以影响生态发展的密度。

transcript.en: Arya: Then what about other approaches?
transcript.cn: 那其他方法呢？

transcript.en: Arya: Is there a quicker way to control it?
transcript.cn: 有没有更快的控制办法？

transcript.en: John: Well, the mechanical removal of water hyacinth is seen as the best short-term solution.
transcript.cn: 嗯，机械清除水葫芦被认为是最好的短期解决方案。

transcript.en: John: This option includes harvesting plants and in-site cutting.
transcript.cn: 这种方式包括收割植物和就地切割。

transcript.en: John: This method can immediately open physical space for fish and boat traffic.
transcript.cn: 这种方法能立刻为鱼类和船只通行腾出物理空间。

transcript.en: John: It is, however, inefficient for workers to process extensive areas, and costly to use large cutting and dredging equipment.
transcript.cn: 不过，让工人去处理大面积区域效率很低，而且使用大型切割和疏浚设备成本也很高。

transcript.en: John: Ironically, it costs even more to dispose of this plant than to remove it.
transcript.cn: 讽刺的是，处理掉这些植物比清除它们花的钱还要多。

transcript.en: Arya: Can we make use of this weed and turn it into something good?
transcript.cn: 我们能不能利用这种杂草，把它变成有用的东西？

transcript.en: John: Now scientists think that water hyacinth can be useful.
transcript.cn: 现在科学家认为水葫芦是有用处的。

transcript.en: John: The plant itself, although more than 95% water, has a fibrous tissue and a high energy and protein content, and can be used for a variety of useful applications.
transcript.cn: 这种植物本身虽然含水量超过95%，但有纤维组织，能量和蛋白质含量都很高，可以用于多种有用的用途。

transcript.en: Arya: So what do people do with water hyacinth that is initially removed?
transcript.cn: 那么，对最初被清除掉的水葫芦，人们会拿它来做什么？

transcript.en: John: First, the cut-down water hyacinth is left to dry under the sun for a few days before being mixed with ash and animal manure.
transcript.cn: 首先，割下来的水葫芦要放在太阳下晒几天，然后和草木灰、动物粪便混合。

transcript.en: John: The mixture can be used as alternative soil rather than fertiliser.
transcript.cn: 这种混合物可以用作替代土壤，而不是作为肥料。

transcript.en: John: It can grow crops with increased yield.
transcript.cn: 它能让作物长得更好、产量更高。

transcript.en: Arya: That sounds interesting.
transcript.cn: 听起来挺有意思的。

transcript.en: John: Mushroom farmers can benefit from water hyacinth as well.
transcript.cn: 种蘑菇的农户也能从水葫芦中受益。

transcript.en: John: Using the mixture mentioned earlier, mushrooms generally grow much faster so that farmers can harvest mushrooms sooner, which enables them to make money faster.
transcript.cn: 用前面提到的那种混合物，蘑菇通常长得快得多，农户就能更早采收蘑菇，也就能更快赚到钱。

transcript.en: Arya: I see.
transcript.cn: 原来如此。

transcript.en: Arya: It can be used to grow oyster and straw mushrooms, right?
transcript.cn: 它可以用来种平菇和草菇，对吧？

transcript.en: John: Yes.
transcript.cn: 是的。

transcript.en: John: In India, attempts have been made to grow these types of edible mushrooms organically utilizing water hyacinth.
transcript.cn: 在印度，人们已经尝试利用水葫芦有机地种植这几种食用菌。

transcript.en: John: These mushrooms provide sufficient nutritional values including minerals and high-quality protein which are essential to people's level of fitness.
transcript.cn: 这些蘑菇能提供充足的营养价值，包括矿物质和优质蛋白，这些对人们的健康水平都很重要。

transcript.en: Arya: Right.
transcript.cn: 对。

transcript.en: Arya: Are there any other measures taken to utilise water hyacinth?
transcript.cn: 还有没有采取其他措施来利用水葫芦呢？

transcript.en: John: In South-east Asia, people feed cows using chopped water hyacinth mixed with other vegetables.
transcript.cn: 在东南亚，人们用切碎的水葫芦拌上其他蔬菜来喂牛。

transcript.en: John: Then the waste from cows is treated to produce methane gas, which can be used as fuel for cooking, lighting or powering an engine.
transcript.cn: 然后牛的排泄物经过处理产生沼气，可以用作烹饪、照明或驱动发动机的燃料。

transcript.en: John: And with global warming getting worse, developing alternative sources of energy will be more urgent than ever.
transcript.cn: 而且随着全球变暖越来越严重，开发替代能源会比以往任何时候都更加紧迫。

transcript.en: Arya: Maybe in the future people will love water hyacinth instead of hating it.
transcript.cn: 也许将来人们会喜欢上水葫芦，而不是讨厌它。

transcript.en: John: Yes, that's very likely if we can make full use of this weed.
transcript.cn: 是的，如果我们能充分利用这种杂草，那很有可能。

groups.title: Nanotechnology and Its Applications
groups.desc:
Questions 31-40
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.collect: About Nanotechnology • It is a study on the scale of nanometre. • The development in nanotechnology is the result of a new type of $31$. • People are worried that nanotech products are $32$. Food and Household • The availability of $33$ in our diet (e.g. in potatoes) is greatly improved. • The packaging cost of $34$ is particularly reduced. • Nanotechnology can increase the $35$ of food. • Nanotechnology can be used to make new types of furniture. Agriculture • The efficiency of $36$ is increased. Health and Medicine • Nanotubes allow more $37$ to enter veins. • As the most popular material, $38$ is used to kill bacteria. • Nanotechnology is also helpful in $39$ loss programs. Cosmetics • Nanoparticles protect skin from the $40$ and help to improve the ability against aging. • Nanotechnology helps to deliver effective elements to the skin.

questions.title: The development in nanotechnology is the result of a new type of 【   】.
questions.analysis:
听前预测：提示词 of，此处应填名词。
题目解析：本题没有很好的定位词，可通过题干之前已给信息中的 nanometre 进行定位。题干信息较录音原文变化大，做题难度相应提升。这要求考生平时不光要掌握一定量的学术性词汇，也要注意总结出题规律，比如本题中 atoms 和 molecules 并列出现，但题目要求仅填写一个单词，因此不会是答案。答案句为："… with the invention of a special kind of microscope, that modern nanotechnology truly began." 其中 modern nanotechnology truly began 对应题干中的 The development in nanotechnology，the invention of a special kind of 对应 a new type of。因此答案为 microscope。

questions.title: People are worried that nanotech products are 【   】.
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 products；提示词 are，此处应填形容词性词汇。
题目解析：本题答案前置，需仔细听取信息。在上一题答案句之后听到："… there is always concern over these unnatural products." 其中 products 原词重现，there is always concern over 替换了题干中的 worried that。因此答案为 unnatural。

questions.title: The availability of 【   】 in our diet (e.g. in potatoes) is greatly improved.
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 Food、potatoes；提示词 of，此处应填名词。
题目解析：本题有一定难度，同样属于答案前置。通过题干小标题可以判断，第 33 至 40 题考查的是纳米科技在各行各业的应用，小标题出现后的内容要集中注意力听。本题 Food 出现后，听到："… nanotechnology is leveraged against iron deficiency which triggers anaemia." 随后 For example 引出了 potatoes 这个例子："… by reducing the particle size in potatoes, this new technology improves the bioavailability of this nutritional ingredient …" 其中 potatoes 原词重现，bioavailability 替换了题干中的 availability，而 this nutritional ingredient 指代的就是前一句的 iron。因此答案为 iron。

questions.title: The packaging cost of 【   】 is particularly reduced.
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 packaging；提示词 of，此处应填名词。
题目解析：本题定位词 packaging 原词重现，可锁定答案句："In terms of packaging, nanomaterials are more economical and are used in all sorts of products, chocolate in particular." more economical 对应题干中的 cost … is reduced，in particular 替换了 particularly。因此答案为 chocolate。

questions.title: Nanotechnology can increase the 【   】 of food.
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 food；提示词 the，此处应填名词。
题目解析：本题定位词 food 定位能力较弱，因为作为小标题，它可能会反复出现。本题通过逻辑关系及词性抓取答案更为可行。第 35 题与上下都为并列关系，并列信号词 also 暗示答案信息即将出现。题干表述与录音原文几乎一样，难度较低。其中 enhance 替换了题干中的 increase，抓取其后名词即可。因此答案为 flavour 或 flavor，英美式拼写都可以。

questions.title: The efficiency of 【   】 is increased.
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 Agriculture；提示词 of，此处应填名词。
题目解析：本题题干信息定位能力较弱，可通过小标题进行定位。定位词 Agriculture 出现后，听到："It offers great potential to strengthen the effect of fertilisers …" 其中 strengthen 替换了题干中的 is increased，effect 替换了 efficiency。因此答案为 fertilisers 或 fertilizers。

questions.title: Nanotubes allow more 【   】 to enter veins.
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 Nanotubes、veins；提示词 allow、more，此处应填名词。
题目解析：本题难度较大，主要是录音中出现了一些医学术语，挑战考生的词汇能力。Nanotubes 中的 tube 意为"管道"，veins 意为"静脉"。通过这两个定位词可锁定答案句："Such nanotubes have become increasingly attractive to researchers because of their ability to efficiently deliver drugs into veins." 其中 their ability to deliver … into 替换了题干中的 allow … to enter。题干中的 more 对应后一句"This means that higher doses of drugs loaded on them can be transported …"中的 higher doses of，答案词的反复出现也意味着强调。因此答案为 drugs。

questions.title: As the most popular material, 【   】 is used to kill bacteria.
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 material、bacteria；提示词 material，此处应填一种表示材料的名词。
题目解析：知识面广的考生或许知道纳米银具有杀菌作用，但注意本题要填的是一种材料。先听到含有定位词 material 的句子，其中出现了 wood、plastic 和 glass 这些材料，它们并列出现，由于题目字数限制，不可能是答案。接着听到了 silver 单独出现，随后便听到 kill some microbes，其中 microbes 替换了题干中的 bacteria。因此答案为 silver。

questions.title: Nanotechnology is also helpful in 【   】 loss programs.
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 loss programs；提示词 in、loss programs，此处应填名词或形容词。
题目解析：通过定位词可锁定答案句："Therefore, treatment programs are provided for patients to lose weight by disposing of excess fat in a relatively safer manner." 其中 programs 前置重现，动词 lose 对应题干名词 loss。因此答案为 weight。注意不能写成同音词 wait，要结合语境判断所填词的合理性。

questions.title: Nanoparticles protect skin from the 【   】 and help to improve the ability against aging.
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 skin、aging；提示词 the，此处应填名词。
题目解析：注意集中注意力听小标题 Cosmetics 出现之后的内容。本题定位词 skin 和 aging 出现在同一句中，很容易可锁定答案句："The first one is that nanoparticles are commonly used as UV filters in anti-aging suncare products to avoid skin damage caused by the sun." 其中 avoid B caused by A 替换了题干中的 protect B from A，B 对应 skin。因此答案为 sun。平时注意积累知识背景：防晒可抗衰老，而皮肤衰老往往是因为太阳光的照射。

transcript.en: Today's lecture is the first of a series of lectures on nanotechnology.
transcript.cn: 今天这节课是纳米技术系列讲座的第一讲。

transcript.en: Nanotechnology, sometimes shortened to 'nanotech', refers to the manipulation of matter on an atomic and molecular level.
transcript.cn: 纳米技术，有时简称为'纳米科技'，指的是在原子和分子层面对物质进行操控。

transcript.en: It's a science, engineering and technology conducted at the nanoscale, which is about 1 to 100 nanometres.
transcript.cn: 它是一门在纳米尺度上开展的科学、工程和技术，纳米尺度大约是1到100纳米。

transcript.en: It is the study and application of extremely small things and can be used across all the other science fields.
transcript.cn: 它研究和应用极其微小的东西，可以运用到其他所有科学领域。

transcript.en: Just how small is that?
transcript.cn: 那到底有多小呢？

transcript.en: A nanometre is one-billionth of a metre.
transcript.cn: 一纳米是一米的十亿分之一。

transcript.en: The idea of nanoscale research first started from a physicist called Richard Feynman in 1959.
transcript.cn: 纳米尺度研究这个想法，最早是由一位名叫理查德·费曼的物理学家在1959年提出的。

transcript.en: He envisioned a process in which scientists would be able to manipulate and control individual atoms and molecules.
transcript.cn: 他设想了一个过程，让科学家能够操控和控制单个的原子和分子。

transcript.en: In fact, it wasn't until 1981, with the invention of a special kind of microscope, that modern nanotechnology truly began.
transcript.cn: 事实上，直到1981年一种特殊显微镜发明之后，现代纳米技术才真正起步。

transcript.en: Even though nanotechnology has created many new materials and devices with a vast range of applications, there is always concern over these unnatural products.
transcript.cn: 尽管纳米技术已经创造出许多应用范围极广的新材料和新设备，但人们始终对这些非天然的产品心存担忧。

transcript.en: Many people are worried that they may pose a threat to the environment, health and safety, also known as EHS.
transcript.cn: 很多人担心它们可能会对环境、健康和安全构成威胁，也就是所谓的EHS。

transcript.en: In order to better understand and evaluate the potential harm, risk assessment has been introduced.
transcript.cn: 为了更好地理解和评估潜在的危害，人们引入了风险评估。

transcript.en: There are also ongoing international collaborations on nanoEHS research and related policy issues.
transcript.cn: 在纳米EHS研究以及相关政策问题上，也有持续的国际合作。

transcript.en: Despite the concern, nanotechnology has benefited society in unexpected ways.
transcript.cn: 尽管存在这些担忧，纳米技术还是以意想不到的方式造福了社会。

transcript.en: In the food processing industry, nanotechnology is leveraged against iron deficiency which triggers anaemia.
transcript.cn: 在食品加工行业，纳米技术被用来对抗会引发贫血的缺铁问题。

transcript.en: For example, by reducing the particle size in potatoes, this new technology improves the bioavailability of this nutritional ingredient without the tendency to cause colour and odour changes.
transcript.cn: 比如说，通过缩小土豆中的颗粒尺寸，这项新技术提高了这种营养成分的生物利用率，而且不会导致颜色和气味发生变化。

transcript.en: In terms of packaging, nanomaterials are more economical and are used in all sorts of products, chocolate in particular.
transcript.cn: 在包装方面，纳米材料更加经济实惠，被用在各种各样的产品上，尤其是巧克力。

transcript.en: Nanotechnology can also enhance the flavour of food.
transcript.cn: 纳米技术还能提升食物的风味。

transcript.en: On a domestic scale, we expect more 'smart' furniture, which is made of shape memory alloys, to be manufactured.
transcript.cn: 在家居层面，我们预计会有更多用形状记忆合金制造的'智能'家具被生产出来。

transcript.en: But nanotechnology has much wider application than this and could also help develop sustainable agriculture.
transcript.cn: 但纳米技术的应用远不止于此，它还能帮助发展可持续农业。

transcript.en: It offers great potential to strengthen the effect of fertilisers, thereby minimising the environmental impact and boosting the productivity of plants in the soil.
transcript.cn: 它极有潜力增强肥料的效果，从而把对环境的影响降到最低，并提高土壤中植物的产量。

transcript.en: In the area of medicine, bioengineers have developed simple and inexpensive nanoscale delivery vehicles.
transcript.cn: 在医学领域，生物工程师已经研发出简单又便宜的纳米级递送载体。

transcript.en: Conductors like carbon nanotubes have been invented with varying structures.
transcript.cn: 像碳纳米管这样的导体已经被制造出来，结构多种多样。

transcript.en: Such nanotubes have become increasingly attractive to researchers because of their ability to efficiently deliver drugs into veins.
transcript.cn: 这类纳米管越来越受研究人员青睐，因为它们能高效地把药物送入静脉。

transcript.en: This means that higher doses of drugs loaded on them can be transported from the administration sites to the effects-related sites, such as cancer focuses, thus decreasing toxic side effects while increasing the treatment effects.
transcript.cn: 这意味着，装载在它们上面的更高剂量的药物，可以从给药部位输送到起效部位，比如癌症病灶，从而在增强治疗效果的同时减少毒副作用。

transcript.en: The research team has also developed a widely accepted material that can be used to coat different surfaces, including wood, plastic and glass.
transcript.cn: 研究团队还研发出一种被广泛认可的材料，可以用来涂覆各种表面，包括木头、塑料和玻璃。

transcript.en: It is mainly comprised of silver.
transcript.cn: 它主要由银构成。

transcript.en: Such nanoparticles can eventually kill some microbes due to the antiseptic properties.
transcript.cn: 这类纳米颗粒因为有杀菌特性，最终能够杀死一些微生物。

transcript.en: The whole process is simple, easy to apply, and more importantly, harmless to the environment.
transcript.cn: 整个过程简单、易于操作，更重要的是，对环境无害。

transcript.en: Surprisingly, nanotechnology has major implications for our health as well.
transcript.cn: 令人惊讶的是，纳米技术对我们的健康也有重大意义。

transcript.en: By changing the metabolic rate through a particular type of protein, obese patients are able to burn calories faster even under a high-calorie diet.
transcript.cn: 通过某种特定蛋白质来改变新陈代谢速率，肥胖患者即使在高热量饮食下也能更快地燃烧卡路里。

transcript.en: Therefore, treatment programs are provided for patients to lose weight by disposing of excess fat in a relatively safer manner.
transcript.cn: 因此，可以为患者提供治疗方案，以相对更安全的方式清除多余脂肪来减重。

transcript.en: The finding may help explain why overweight people struggle to lose weight.
transcript.cn: 这一发现或许能解释，为什么超重的人减肥这么困难。

transcript.en: Their stored fat is actively fighting against their efforts to burn it off at the molecular level.
transcript.cn: 他们储存的脂肪正在分子层面上，积极对抗他们想把它燃烧掉的努力。

transcript.en: Further research is mainly focused on the clinical and commercial development of therapies for obesity, diabetes and other associated conditions such as heart disease.
transcript.cn: 进一步的研究主要集中在针对肥胖、糖尿病以及心脏病等其他相关病症的疗法的临床和商业开发上。

transcript.en: In terms of cosmetics, the applications of nanotechnology and nanomaterials can be found in many products.
transcript.cn: 在化妆品方面，纳米技术和纳米材料的应用可以在许多产品中找到。

transcript.en: There are two main uses for nanotechnology in cosmetics.
transcript.cn: 纳米技术在化妆品中主要有两大用途。

transcript.en: The first one is that nanoparticles are commonly used as UV filters in anti-aging suncare products to avoid skin damage caused by the sun.
transcript.cn: 第一种是把纳米颗粒作为抗衰老防晒护理产品中的紫外线过滤剂，用来避免阳光对皮肤造成的伤害。

transcript.en: In addition, nanotechnology has played an important role in delivering active ingredients to the skin.
transcript.cn: 此外，纳米技术在把有效成分递送到皮肤方面也发挥了重要作用。

transcript.en: The mechanism proves to improve the bioavailability of actives and enhance skin hydration.
transcript.cn: 事实证明，这一机制能提高有效成分的生物利用率，增强皮肤的保湿效果。

transcript.en: The wide variety of nanotechnology applications leave no doubt that this field will only continue to get more exciting, and I look forward to seeing these developments further branch out.
transcript.cn: 纳米技术应用之广泛，让人毫不怀疑这一领域只会变得越来越精彩，我也很期待看到这些发展进一步开枝散叶。
